Items.Title: TypeScript 可选参数 | TypeScript Optional Parameters
Items.Description:
在 Javascript 中，如果一个函数有 4 个参数，而我们只使用 3 个参数，这没什么大不了的 - Javascript 只是忽略缺少的参数并像undefined一样运行函数。

在 TypeScript 中，情况有些不同。 如果您尝试使用与函数实际具有的参数数量不同的参数运行函数，则会收到错误消息。 例如，考虑一下：

 const myFunction = ( a : string , b : string , c ?: string ) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c ; } let runFunction = myFunction ( " Hello " , " there " );

上面的代码会抛出以下错误：

 Expected 3 arguments, but got 2.

因此，我们需要明确告诉 TypeScript，我们在函数上定义的一个或多个参数是可选的。 为了做到这一点，我们使用? 在参数之后，表示它实际上是一个可选参数。

例如，如果我们想在我们的原始函数上使c成为可选，我们可以编写以下内容 - 而不会出现错误：

 const myFunction = ( a : string , b : string , c ?: string ) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c ; } let runFunction = myFunction ( " Hello " , " there " ); console . log ( runFunction );

现在我们的函数将按预期运行，但是输出将是Hello there undefined 。 这可能不是您想要的行为，这正是 TypeScript 拥有这些控件的原因 - 它可以通过要求某些参数来帮助防止不需要的结果。

然而，在 TypeScript 中，可选参数后面不能跟必填参数。 因此，例如，以下代码将不起作用：

 const myFunction = ( a : string , b ?: string , c : string ) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c ; }

它将产生以下错误：

 A required parameter cannot follow an optional parameter.

我们可以将此可选性与typeof 运算符的使用结合起来，以生成有意义的代码。 例如，如果c是可选的且未定义，那么我们可以忽略它，如下所示：

 const myFunction = ( a : string , b : string , c ?: string ) => { if ( typeof c === undefined ) { return a + " " + b ; } return a + " " + b + " " + c ; } let runFunction = myFunction ( " Hello " , " there " ); console . log ( runFunction );

现在我们的代码输出将是Hello there ，但我们也可以使用c运行它以产生不同的输出，例如：

 const myFunction = ( a : string , b : string , c ?: string ) => { if ( typeof c === undefined ) { return a + " " + b ; } return a + " " + b + " " + c ; } let runFunction = myFunction ( " How " , " are " , " you? " ); // Returns "How are you?" console . log ( runFunction );

穆罕默德·米尔法特米—— 阿舍尔巴德·帕尼格拉希 - 一旦暂停，smpnjn 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，smpnjn 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，smpnjn 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 smpnjn 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

In Javascript, if a function has 4 parameters, and we only use 3, it's no big deal - Javascript just ignores the missing parameter and runs the function as if it is undefined.

In TypeScript, things are a little different. If you try to run a function with a different number of parameters than the function actually has, you'll get an error. For example, consider this:

const myFunction = (a: string, b: string, c?: string) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c;
} let runFunction = myFunction("Hello", "there");

The above code will throw the following error:

Expected 3 arguments, but got 2.

TypeScript does this to ensure fewer errors, since leaving a parameter out of a function you are calling can sometimes break the function. As such, we need to tell TypeScript specifically that one or more of the parameters we defined on the function are optional. In order to do that, we use a ? after the parameter, to denote that it is actually an optional parameter.

For example, if we wanted to make c optional on our original function, we could write the following - and not get an error:

const myFunction = (a: string, b: string, c?: string) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c;
} let runFunction = myFunction("Hello", "there"); console.log(runFunction);

Now our function will run as expected, however the output will be Hello there undefined. This may not be your desired behaviour, which is exactly why TypeScript has these controls in place - it can help prevent unwanted outcomes by requiring certain parameters.

In TypeScript, however, an optional parameter cannot be followed by a required one. So for example, the following code will not work:

const myFunction = (a: string, b?: string, c: string) => { return a + " " + b + " " + c;
}

It will produce the following error:

A required parameter cannot follow an optional parameter.

We can combine this optionality with the use of the typeof operator to produce code that makes sense. For example, if c is optional and undefined, then we can ignore it, like so:

const myFunction = (a: string, b: string, c?: string) => { if(typeof c === undefined) { return a + " " + b; } return a + " " + b + " " + c;
} let runFunction = myFunction("Hello", "there"); console.log(runFunction);

Now the output of our code will be Hello there, but we could also run it with c to produce a different output, for example:

const myFunction = (a: string, b: string, c?: string) => { if(typeof c === undefined) { return a + " " + b; } return a + " " + b + " " + c;
} let runFunction = myFunction("How", "are", "you?"); // Returns "How are you?"
console.log(runFunction);

Mohammad Mirfatemi - Jul 3

Ashirbad Panigrahi - Jul 7

Once suspended, smpnjn will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, smpnjn will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by smpnjn will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If smpnjn is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Items.Title: 我应该先学习什么编程语言？ | What programming language should I learn first?
Items.Description:
您是否感到不知所措，不知道应该先学习哪种编程语言？ 好吧，你来对地方了。

在这篇文章中，我们将列出一些最流行的编程语言，并解释你可以用它们做什么。 这篇文章的目的是帮助您选择一种语言并开始编码。

学习编程概念比学习任何特定的编程语言重要得多。 那是因为这些概念独立于编程语言。 如果您想了解这方面的介绍，请随时下载我们的电子书。

让我们从列表开始。

注意：为了便于比较，所有代码示例都代表了用不同语言编写的大致相同的程序。

它有大量的库，可用于行业的各个分支。 它在 QA 自动化任务中也非常受欢迎。

这是一种非常容易学习的语言：它使用缩进而不是花括号，并且不需要使用分号。 由于它易于学习，因此它通常是非程序员（如科学家、机械工程师、会计师等）的首选语言。

它看起来像什么？

 import json data = json . loads ( '{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }' ) sample = { "blue" : [ 1 , 2 ], "ocean" : "water" } json_string = json . dumps ( sample ) print ( json_string )

它是干什么用的？ 数据科学、数据可视化、机器学习、任务自动化、Web 开发（服务器端）、游戏开发等。

我在哪里可以学习它？ 使用 Python 自动化枯燥的工作- 一个学习 Python 的绝佳在线资源。 它介绍了 Python，然后向您展示了如何解决一些日常任务。

它用于开发交互式网页或应用程序。 大约 97% 的页面在客户端使用 JS 来控制网页行为。 它通常与 HTML 和 CSS 结合使用。

它还拥有大量库，可帮助您轻松解决各种类型的任务。

它看起来像什么？

 var data = JSON . parse ( ' { "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] } ' ); var sample = { " blue " : [ 1 , 2 ], " ocean " : " water " }; var json_string = JSON . stringify ( sample ); console . log ( json_string )

它是干什么用的？ 前端 Web 开发、后端 Web 开发（Node.js）、游戏开发（浏览器游戏）、移动应用程序（React Native）

我在哪里可以学习它？ 我们还需要多说吗？

爪哇

它曾经并且仍然是一种非常流行的语言，主要是因为 Java 虚拟机能够在许多不同类型的设备上运行 Java 代码。 它的语法类似于 C 和 C++，但与它们不同的是，它不是低级语言，这使得它对初学者更加友好。

它看起来像什么？

 import com.google.gson.Gson ; public class JsonExample { public static void main ( String [] args ) { Gson gson = new Gson (); String json = "{ \"foo\": 1, \"bar\": [ \"10\", \"apples\"] }" ; MyJsonObject obj = gson . fromJson ( json , MyJsonObject . class ); System . out . println ( obj . getFoo ()); for ( String bar : obj . getBar ()) { System . out . println ( bar ); } obj = new MyJsonObject ( 2 , new String [] { "20" , "oranges" }); json = gson . toJson ( obj ); System . out . println ( json ); } }

它是干什么用的？ 我在哪里可以学习它？ 它类似于 Java，被认为是它的直接竞争对手。 如果不涉及不必要的细节，没有什么可添加的。

它看起来像什么？

 using System ; using System.Collections.Generic ; using System.Web.Script.Serialization ; class Program { static void Main () { var people = new Dictionary < string , object > {{ "1" , "John" }, { "2" , "Susan" }}; var serializer = new JavaScriptSerializer (); var json = serializer . Serialize ( people ); Console . WriteLine ( json ); var deserialized = serializer . Deserialize < Dictionary < string , object >>( json ); Console . WriteLine ( deserialized [ "2" ]); var jsonObject = serializer . DeserializeObject ( @"{ ""foo"": 1, ""bar"": [10, ""apples""] }" ); var data = jsonObject as Dictionary < string , object >; var array = data [ "bar" ] as object []; Console . WriteLine ( array [ 1 ]); } }

它是干什么用的？ 我在哪里可以学习它？ 微软的官方页面- 它有一堆视频教程和文档

去

它能够以非阻塞方式支持并发，当您需要运行多个并发进程时，它可以扩展。 它仍然是一种相对年轻的语言，可能有其缺点。 然而，许多人喜欢使用 Go 的简单性，越来越多的公司开始在他们的一些流程中使用它。

它看起来像什么？

 package main import "encoding/json" import "fmt" func main () { var data interface {} err := json . Unmarshal ([] byte ( `{"foo":1, "bar":[10, "apples"]}` ), & data ) if err == nil { fmt . Println ( data ) } else { fmt . Println ( err ) } sample := map [ string ] interface {}{ "blue" : [] interface {}{ 1 , 2 }, "ocean" : "water" , } json_string , err := json . Marshal ( sample ) if err == nil { fmt . Println ( string ( json_string )) } else { fmt . Println ( err ) } }

它是干什么用的？ 基础设施、Web 开发（服务器端）、数据科学、机器学习

我在哪里可以学习它？ 准备好学习编程了吗？

在这篇简短的文章中，我们为您提供了一些指导，帮助您选择您的第一种编程语言。 决定上面的编程路径之一，然后选择一种语言来配合它。

如果您无法决定，那么我们建议您选择 Python。 这是清单上最容易学习的一种，您将有很好的机会找到一份工作。

如果您对每种语言的流行程度以及使用该语言的开发人员的平均薪水感兴趣，请查看StackOverflow 2022 调查。

你在等什么？ 选择一种语言并开始！ 在评论中让我们知道您选择了哪一个。



原文:

Are you feeling overwhelmed and don't know which programming language you should learn first? Well, you've come to the right place.

In this post, we'll list some of the most popular programming languages and explain what you can do with them. The goal of this post is to help you pick one of the languages and start coding.

Learning programming concepts is much more important than learning any specific programming language. That's because the concepts are independent of programming languages. If you'd like an intro on that, feel free to download our e-book.

Let's start with the list.

Note: All the code examples represent roughly the same program written in different languages for easy comparison.

Python

Python is one of the most popular programming languages in the last decade, especially for beginners.

It has a large number of libraries that can be used for various branches of the industry. It's also extremely popular for QA automation tasks.

It's a very easy language to learn: it uses indentation instead of curly braces and it does not need to use semicolons. Since it's easy to learn, it's often the language of choice for people that are not programmers - like scientists, mechanical engineers, accountants, etc.

What it looks like?

import json
data = json.loads('{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }') sample = { "blue": [1,2], "ocean": "water" }
json_string = json.dumps(sample) print(json_string)

What's it used for? data science, data visualization, machine learning, task automation, web development (server-side), game development, etc.

Where can I learn it? Automate the boring stuff with Python - a great online resource to learn Python from. It has an introduction to Python after which it shows you how to solve some everyday tasks.

JavaScript

JavaScript or JS is the programming language that some people love and some people hate. It's used to develop interactive web pages or applications. About 97% of pages use JS on the client-side to control web page behavior. It's usually used in combination with HTML and CSS.

It also has a large number of libraries that help you easily solve various types of tasks.

What it looks like?

var data = JSON.parse('{ "foo": 1, "bar": [10, "apples"] }'); var sample = { "blue": [1,2], "ocean": "water" };
var json_string = JSON.stringify(sample); console.log(json_string)

What's it used for? frontend web development, backend web development (Node.js), game development (browser games), mobile applications (React Native)

Where can I learn it? The Odin Project - a page that offers a full-stack JavaScript path for free. Do we need to say more?

Java

(Do not confuse Java with JavaScript, they are not similar at all)

Java is another versatile programming language. It was and still is a pretty popular language, mainly because of the Java virtual machine that enables to run Java code on many different types of devices. Its syntax is similar to C and C++, but unlike them, it's not a low-level language, which makes it much more friendly to beginners.

What it looks like?

import com.google.gson.Gson; public class JsonExample { public static void main(String[] args) { Gson gson = new Gson(); String json = "{ \"foo\": 1, \"bar\": [ \"10\", \"apples\"] }"; MyJsonObject obj = gson.fromJson(json, MyJsonObject.class); System.out.println(obj.getFoo()); for(String bar : obj.getBar()) { System.out.println(bar); } obj = new MyJsonObject(2, new String[] { "20", "oranges" }); json = gson.toJson(obj); System.out.println(json); } }

What's it used for? web development (server-side) - good for large enterprise applications, desktop applications (Windows and Linux), mobile development (Android apps - however, Kotlin is now more popular for Android developers)

Where can I learn it? Programiz - a site that explains Java from scratch

C#

C# is a programming language developed by Microsoft. It's similar to Java and is considered to be its direct rival. There is nothing much to add without going into unnecessary details.

What it looks like?

using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Web.Script.Serialization; class Program
{ static void Main() { var people = new Dictionary<string, object> {{"1", "John"}, {"2", "Susan"}}; var serializer = new JavaScriptSerializer(); var json = serializer.Serialize(people); Console.WriteLine(json); var deserialized = serializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, object>>(json); Console.WriteLine(deserialized["2"]); var jsonObject = serializer.DeserializeObject(@"{ ""foo"": 1, ""bar"": [10, ""apples""] }"); var data = jsonObject as Dictionary<string, object>; var array = data["bar"] as object[]; Console.WriteLine(array[1]); }
}

What's it used for? web development (server-side), desktop applications (Windows), game development (Unity), mobile development (Xamarin)

Where can I learn it? Microsoft's official page - it has a bunch of video tutorials and documentation

Go

Go or Golang is a fast and easy-to-learn programming language developed by a team at Google. It has the ability to support concurrency in a non-blocking way which makes it scalable when you need to run multiple concurrent processes. It's still a relatively young language, which can have its disadvantages. However, many people love using Go for its simplicity and more and more companies are starting to use it for some of their processes.

What it looks like?

package main import "encoding/json"
import "fmt" func main() { var data interface{} err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(`{"foo":1, "bar":[10, "apples"]}`), &data) if err == nil { fmt.Println(data) } else { fmt.Println(err) } sample := map[string]interface{}{ "blue": []interface{}{1, 2}, "ocean": "water", } json_string, err := json.Marshal(sample) if err == nil { fmt.Println(string(json_string)) } else { fmt.Println(err) }
}

What's it used for? infrastructure, web development (server-side), data science, machine learning

Where can I learn it? Golang BootCamp - a book that has everything you need to know to start with Golang

Ready to learn programming?

In this short post we gave you some directions which will help you choose your first programming language. Decide on one of the programming paths above and then pick a language to go with it.

If you can't decide, then we'd suggest you pick Python. It's the easiest one to learn on the list and you'll have a good chance finding a job with it.

If you're interested in the popularity of each language and what's the average salary of developers working in that language, then check out the StackOverflow 2022 survey.

What are you waiting for? Pick one language and start! Let us know in the comments which one you've picked.
